name: shallowswe-repair-loop-preview-n3-18-v0.2-config-rollover-temp

selection_policy.purpose: Preview run with one newly calibrated large replacement per category after the July 5 floor recalibration showed the original six large slots no longer retained large-band behavior. Temporary continuation excludes incident-comms-pipeline after it behaved as an over-hard outlier; config-key-rollover is restored for the workflow large slot pending tomorrow review.

large_replacements.reason: status-terminal-parity saturated at 9/9 in the July 5 floor recalibration; invoice-multi-source-merge produced large-band one-shot pressure.
support-metrics-package saturated at 9/9 in the July 5 floor recalibration; support-sla-business-hours produced large-band one-shot pressure.
config-key-rollover saturated at 9/9 in the July 5 floor recalibration; incident-comms-pipeline produced large-band one-shot pressure after verifier repairs.

notes: This is still a preview plan, not final snapshot admission.
The plan keeps one older large-labeled placeholder per category to preserve the 18-task matrix while the remaining large slots are refilled.
The three replacement tasks are marked candidate_probe in task metadata because this plan is explicit-task selection, not formal calibrated snapshot admission.
Temporary July 6 continuation: incident-comms-pipeline rows are retained locally but excluded from website/export aggregates; config-key-rollover rows are used in the workflow-large slot.

temporary_replacement.reason: incident-comms-pipeline produced 1/10 pass behavior in the repair-loop run and is held aside for investigation.

notes: This is a feedback-seeking preview run, not a final ShallowSWE snapshot.
Final publish-grade scoring remains N=10 repair-loop seeds per task/model_config, with N=20 only for disputed frontier cells.
Run validation should inspect preliminary trajectories immediately and stop early on verifier/task unfairness before retrying.
